```json
{
  "engineeringPlan": ["Implement gameplay mechanics"],
  "commands": [
    {
      "type": "insert_script",
      "payload": {
        "scriptId": "CharacterMovementScript",
        "scriptType": "LocalScript",
        "parent": "StarterPlayerScripts",
        "code": "local Players = game:GetService('Players')
local player = Players.LocalPlayer
local character = player.Character
local humanoid = character:WaitForChild('Humanoid')

local function moveCharacter(direction)
    if direction == 'forward' then
        humanoid:MoveTo(character.HumanoidRootPart.Position + character.HumanoidRootPart.CFrame.LookVector * 10)
    elseif direction == 'backward' then
        humanoid:MoveTo(character.HumanoidRootPart.Position - character.HumanoidRootPart.CFrame.LookVector * 10)
    elseif direction == 'left' then
        humanoid:MoveTo(character.HumanoidRootPart.Position - character.HumanoidRootPart.CFrame.RightVector * 10)
    elseif direction == 'right' then
        humanoid:MoveTo(character.HumanoidRootPart.Position + character.HumanoidRootPart.CFrame.RightVector * 10)
    end
end

game:GetService('UserInputService').InputBegan:Connect(function(input)
    if input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.W then
        moveCharacter('forward')
    elseif input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.S then
        moveCharacter('backward')
    elseif input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.A then
        moveCharacter('left')
    elseif input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.D then
        moveCharacter('right')
    end
end)"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "insert_script",
      "payload": {
        "scriptId": "VehicleDrivingScript",
        "scriptType": "LocalScript",
        "parent": "StarterPlayerScripts",
        "code": "local Players = game:GetService('Players')
local player = Players.LocalPlayer
local character = player.Character
local vehicle = nil

local function driveVehicle(vehicle)
    if vehicle then
        vehicle:SetPrimaryPartCFrame(character.HumanoidRootPart.CFrame * CFrame.new(0, 0, -5))
    end
end

game:GetService('UserInputService').InputBegan:Connect(function(input)
    if input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.E then
        if vehicle then
            driveVehicle(vehicle)
        else
            local nearestVehicle = nil
            local nearestDistance = math.huge
            for _, object in pairs(workspace:GetDescendants()) do
                if object:IsA('VehicleSeat') then
                    local distance = (object.Position - character.HumanoidRootPart.Position).Magnitude
                    if distance < nearestDistance then
                        nearestDistance = distance
                        nearestVehicle = object
                    end
                end
            end
            if nearestVehicle then
                vehicle = nearestVehicle.Parent
                driveVehicle(vehicle)
            end
        end
    end
end)"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "insert_script",
      "payload": {
        "scriptId": "ObjectInteractionScript",
        "scriptType": "LocalScript",
        "parent": "StarterPlayerScripts",
        "code": "local Players = game:GetService('Players')
local player = Players.LocalPlayer
local character = player.Character

local function interactWithObject(object)
    if object:IsA('Part') or object:IsA('MeshPart') then
        print('Interacting with object')
    elseif object:IsA('VehicleSeat') then
        print('Getting into vehicle')
    end
end

game:GetService('UserInputService').InputBegan:Connect(function(input)
    if input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.F then
        local mouse = game:GetService('Players').LocalPlayer:GetMouse()
        local target = mouse.Target
        if target then
            interactWithObject(target)
        end
    end
end)"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "engineeringPlan": ["Implement gameplay mechanics"],
  "commands": [
    {
      "type": "insert_script",
      "payload": {
        "scriptId": "CharacterMovementScript",
        "scriptType": "LocalScript",
        "parent": "StarterPlayerScripts",
        "source": "local Players = game:GetService('Players')
local player = Players.LocalPlayer
local character = player.Character
local humanoid = character:WaitForChild('Humanoid')
local speed = 10
local jumpPower = 50
local isJumping = false

game:GetService('UserInputService').InputBegan:Connect(function(input)
    if input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.W then
        humanoid:MoveTo(character.HumanoidRootPart.Position + character.HumanoidRootPart.CFrame.LookVector * speed)
    elseif input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.S then
        humanoid:MoveTo(character.HumanoidRootPart.Position - character.HumanoidRootPart.CFrame.LookVector * speed)
    elseif input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.A then
        humanoid:MoveTo(character.HumanoidRootPart.Position - character.HumanoidRootPart.CFrame.RightVector * speed)
    elseif input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.D then
        humanoid:MoveTo(character.HumanoidRootPart.Position + character.HumanoidRootPart.CFrame.RightVector * speed)
    elseif input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.Space and not isJumping then
        isJumping = true
        humanoid.JumpPower = jumpPower
        humanoid.Jump = true
        wait(1)
        isJumping = false
    end
end)"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "insert_script",
      "payload": {
        "scriptId": "VehicleDrivingScript",
        "scriptType": "LocalScript",
        "parent": "StarterPlayerScripts",
        "source": "local Players = game:GetService('Players')
local player = Players.LocalPlayer
local character = player.Character
local vehicle = nil
local speed = 20

game:GetService('UserInputService').InputBegan:Connect(function(input)
    if input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.E then
        if vehicle then
            vehicle:SetPrimaryPartCFrame(character.HumanoidRootPart.CFrame * CFrame.new(0, 0, -5))
        else
            local nearestVehicle = nil
            local nearestDistance = math.huge
            for _, object in pairs(workspace:GetDescendants()) do
                if object:IsA('VehicleSeat') then
                    local distance = (object.Position - character.HumanoidRootPart.Position).Magnitude
                    if distance < nearestDistance then
                        nearestDistance = distance
                        nearestVehicle = object
                    end
                end
            end
            if nearestVehicle then
                vehicle = nearestVehicle.Parent
                vehicle:SetPrimaryPartCFrame(character.HumanoidRootPart.CFrame * CFrame.new(0, 0, -5))
            end
        end
    end
end)

game:GetService('RunService').RenderStepped:Connect(function(dt)
    if vehicle then
        local direction = (vehicle.PrimaryPart.CFrame.lookVector)
        vehicle:SetPrimaryPartCFrame(vehicle.PrimaryPart.CFrame + direction * speed * dt)
    end
end)"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "insert_script",
      "payload": {
        "scriptId": "ObjectInteractionScript",
        "scriptType": "LocalScript",
        "parent": "StarterPlayerScripts",
        "source": "local Players = game:GetService('Players')
local player = Players.LocalPlayer
local character = player.Character

game:GetService('UserInputService').InputBegan:Connect(function(input)
    if input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.F then
        local mouse = game:GetService('Players').LocalPlayer:GetMouse()
        local target = mouse.Target
        if target then
            if target:IsA('Part') or target:IsA('MeshPart') then
                print('Interacting with object')
            elseif target:IsA('VehicleSeat') then
                print('Getting into vehicle')
            end
        end
    end
end)"
      }
    }
  ]
}

            end
            if nearestVehicle then
                vehicle = nearestVehicle.Parent
                vehicle:SetPrimaryPartCFrame(character.HumanoidRootPart.CFrame * CFrame.new(0, 0, -5))
            end
        end
    end
end)

game:GetService('RunService').RenderStepped:Connect(function(dt)
    if vehicle then
        local direction = (vehicle.PrimaryPart.CFrame.lookVector)
        vehicle:SetPrimaryPartCFrame(vehicle.PrimaryPart.CFrame + direction * speed * dt)
    end
end)"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "insert_script",
      "payload": {
        "scriptId": "ObjectInteractionScript",
        "scriptType": "LocalScript",
        "parent": "StarterPlayer.StarterPlayerScripts",
        "source": "local Players = game:GetService('Players')
local player = Players.LocalPlayer
local character = player.Character

game:GetService('UserInputService').InputBegan:Connect(function(input)
    if input.KeyCode == Enum.KeyCode.F then
        local mouse = game:GetService('Players').LocalPlayer:GetMouse()
        local target = mouse.Target
        if target then
            if target:IsA('Part') or target:IsA('MeshPart') then
                print('Interacting with object')
            elseif target:IsA('VehicleSeat') then
                print('Getting into vehicle')
            end
        end
    end
end)"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

currentPlan.title: GTA Style Game World
currentPlan.summary: A vast open-world game inspired by Grand Theft Auto, featuring a large city with multiple districts, roads, buildings, and props. The city will be divided into different areas, including a downtown district, a suburban area, an industrial zone, and a waterfront. The game will include various vehicles, characters, and gameplay mechanics.

steps.description: Create a large baseplate with a size of [1024, 1, 1024] to serve as the foundation for the game world, providing ample space for the city and its surroundings. Position it at [0, 0, 0] with a size of [1024, 1, 1024].
Find and insert a 'road' model to create the main highway. Position it at [0, 0.1, 0] with a size of [1000, 0.2, 20]. This will be the central road that connects the different districts of the city.

Create the downtown district by inserting 10+ building models, including skyscrapers, offices, and shops. Position them at various locations, such as [100, 0, 100], [150, 0, 150], [200, 0, 200], and so on, with sizes ranging from [20, 100, 20] to [50, 200, 50]. Space them at least 60 studs apart to create a realistic urban environment.

Add road segments to connect the downtown district to the suburban area. Insert 'road' models at positions such as [300, 0.1, 0], [400, 0.1, 0], and [500, 0.1, 0], with sizes of [200, 0.2, 20], [250, 0.2, 20], and [300, 0.2, 20], respectively.

Create the suburban area by inserting 10+ building models, including houses, apartments, and shops. Position them at various locations, such as [600, 0, 100], [650, 0, 150], [700, 0, 200], and so on, with sizes ranging from [15, 20, 15] to [30, 50, 30]. Space them at least 60 studs apart to create a realistic suburban environment.

Add props to the game world, including vehicles, trees, streetlights, and pedestrians. Insert 'car' models at positions such as [100, 0.1, 100], [200, 0.1, 200], and [300, 0.1, 300], with sizes of [5, 2, 10], [5, 2, 10], and [5, 2, 10], respectively. Insert 'tree' models at positions such as [50, 0, 50], [100, 0, 100], and [150, 0, 150], with sizes of [5, 10, 5], [5, 10, 5], and [5, 10, 5], respectively.

Create the industrial zone by inserting 5+ building models, including factories, warehouses, and offices. Position them at various locations, such as [800, 0, 100], [850, 0, 150], [900, 0, 200], and so on, with sizes ranging from [30, 50, 30] to [50, 100, 50]. Space them at least 60 studs apart to create a realistic industrial environment.

Add a waterfront area to the game world by inserting 'water' models at positions such as [0, 0, 500], [0, 0, 600], and [0, 0, 700], with sizes of [100, 1, 100], [100, 1, 100], and [100, 1, 100], respectively. Insert 'dock' models at positions such as [50, 0, 550], [100, 0, 600], and [150, 0, 650], with sizes of [20, 5, 20], [20, 5, 20], and [20, 5, 20], respectively.
Implement gameplay mechanics, including character movement, vehicle driving, and interaction with objects. This will involve scripting and programming to create a engaging and immersive game world.
Test and refine the game world to ensure that it is stable, functional, and fun to play. This will involve testing the game mechanics, fixing bugs, and making adjustments to the game world as needed.
